project_specification.project_name: YouTube Subtitle to AI Article Converter - MVP
project_specification.overview: This project builds a web application deployed on Cloudflare Worker. Users input a YouTube video link with subtitles, and AI converts the video content into a well-structured Chinese article with a single click. It supports real-time streaming generation, custom generation rules, and chapter-level 5W1H structured summaries. The core product goal is to validate the "video content → structured article" automation workflow, providing content creators and learners with an efficient information organization tool.

frontend.framework: Bundled vanilla JS + CSS via esbuild. No SPA framework — bundled modules for clean separation.
frontend.markdown: Lightweight regex parser for chapter rendering
frontend.stream_rendering: SSE streaming DOM rendering (real-time typewriter effect)
frontend.bundling: esbuild bundles src/frontend/ → public/. Worker serves public/ as static assets.

backend.streaming: TransformStream + SSE (Content-Type: text/event-stream) for real-time AI output
backend.tcp_socket: cloudflare:sockets connect() for Webshare proxy tunneling (Free plan compatible)
backend.security: Encrypted secrets via wrangler.toml or Cloudflare dashboard; no key exposure
backend.modularity: Clear module separation — each file has a single responsibility

youtube_module.library: youtubei.js (npm: youtubei.js) — most active Innertube API library
youtube_module.worker_compat: Risk: youtubei.js may use Node.js APIs incompatible with Workers. Fallback: raw Innertube HTTP calls or a Workers-compatible fork if needed.
youtube_module.language_strategy: zh-CN → zh → en (auto fallback chain) Cloudflare TCP Socket connect() + Webshare.io proxy (anti-captcha) Hard-coded subtitle fallback from src/fallback-subtitles/ (100% availability for demo)

providers.default: Gemini (gemini-2.5-flash) — generateContentStream() + generateContent(). The default AI provider for production deployment.
providers.local_dev: DeepSeek (deepseek-chat) — OpenAI-compatible chat completions API. Takes priority in local development when DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is set (free tier, no credit card required).

ai_module.selection_logic: Env-var auto-detection: if DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is present, all AI calls route to DeepSeek (local dev convenience); otherwise Gemini is the default (production). No runtime fallback between providers — the choice is fixed at Worker startup.
ai_module.streaming: SSE streaming output via DeepSeek chat completions (stream: true) for local dev, or Gemini generateContentStream() by default
ai_module.prompt_engineering: Dual-format prompt templates in src/backend/prompts.ts: plain text for Gemini (buildArticlePrompt / build5W1HPrompt) and chat messages for DeepSeek (buildArticleMessages / build5W1HMessages). Same generation rules, different transport format.
ai_module.response_format: Article text in Markdown + 5W1H structured JSON
ai_module.error_handling: Graceful failure with fallback content; provider-specific error messages (DeepSeek/Gemini distinguished in error output)

storage_module.purpose: Session context cache for 5W1H (no re-transmission of full article from client)
storage_module.data_schema: Type-safe structured session object TTL 3600s (auto cleanup, cost-efficient)

infrastructure.proxy_implementation: TCP Socket tunnel via cloudflare:sockets connect() (Worker-compatible)

prerequisites.item: Cloudflare account (for Worker deployment and KV namespace creation)
Google Gemini API Key (free tier, for gemini-3.5-flash)
Webshare.io free proxy account (for bypassing YouTube anti-bot / captcha)
Node.js + npm environment (local development and dependency installation)

core_features.subtitle_extraction: - Supports standard YouTube link parsing (youtube.com and youtu.be formats) - Prefers Simplified Chinese subtitles; falls back to English when Chinese subtitles are unavailable - Direct mode uses youtubei.js Innertube API to pull subtitles directly - Automatically switches to Webshare TCP Socket proxy mode on captcha / anti-bot trigger - Falls back to built-in hard-coded subtitles from src/fallback-subtitles/ when all else fails - Automatically strips subtitle timestamps and irrelevant markers, outputting clean plain text
core_features.article_generation: - Streaming text generation via gemini-3.5-flash generateContentStream() API - Frontend renders a real-time typewriter effect — content is displayed chunk by chunk as it is generated - Auto-detects Markdown headings (##) for chapter division - Preserves conversational tone while optimizing for readable article layout - Supports optional natural-language generation rules (taskType, style, audience, constraint) sent as `rule: string` in the request body - Shows loading state during generation; provides friendly degradation on failure
core_features.chapter_summary: - Automatically splits the article into chapters based on Markdown ## headings - Chapter titles are normalized: ## prefix and leading/trailing whitespace stripped before storage and matching - Clicking the [5W1H] button next to a chapter sends sessionId + chapter title to server - Server retrieves full article context from KV (no client re-transmission) - Generates structured Who/What/When/Where/Why/How summaries via Gemini - Summaries rendered in a fixed format: Who, What, When, Where, Why, How as structured JSON - Summary boxes are collapsed by default, expand on click
core_features.ui_interaction: - Clean single-page layout divided into input area and result display area - Input area: YouTube link field (required) + custom rules text field (optional) + Generate button - Generate button auto-disabled during requests to prevent duplicate submissions - Article content with automatic word wrapping and reading optimization, responsive to screen widths - Chapter headings clearly distinguished from body text with scroll anchoring - 5W1H summary boxes collapsed by default, expand on click
core_features.error_handling: - Invalid link format validation with instant feedback on input errors - Subtitle fallback chain: direct → proxy → hard-coded (workflow never breaks) - Gemini API failures return clear error messages for easy debugging - Auto-rotates through multiple Webshare proxy nodes in proxy mode - KV session caches auto-expire after 1 hour to prevent storage bloat - After generation completes, session context persists in KV — 5W1H queries work across page refreshes for up to 1 hour

kv_namespace.description: Stores session context data to enable the 5W1H summary feature

api_endpoints_summary.pages: - GET / — Serves the frontend HTML page - GET /public/* — Serves bundled static assets (JS, CSS)
api_endpoints_summary.api: - POST /api/generate — { youtubeUrl, rule? } → SSE stream of article text, X-Session-Id header - POST /api/5w1h — { sessionId, chapter } → JSON { who, what, when, where, why, how }

section.name: Input Area
section.element: YouTube video URL input field (required, with URL format validation)
Custom generation rules text field (optional, natural language: task type, style, audience, constraints)
"Start Generation" button (triggers /api/generate, disabled during active generation)

section.name: Result Display Area
section.element: Streaming article rendering area with Markdown heading (# for title, ## for chapters) and paragraph formatting
"5W1H" button next to each chapter heading (## level)
Collapsible structured summary box per chapter — expands on click, shows Who/What/When/Where/Why/How

color_scheme.primary: #2563eb (blue, used for buttons and emphasis elements)

components.component: Rounded border, light gray background, focus highlight
Hover effect, disabled state styling (grayed out)
Light border, padding to distinguish different chapters
Light gray background, visually distinct from chapter content

interaction.trigger: User enters a YouTube link and clicks "Start Generation"

flow.step: 1. Frontend POSTs to /api/generate with { youtubeUrl, rule? }
2. Server extracts video ID, fetches subtitles: youtubei.js direct → Webshare TCP proxy → src/fallback-subtitles/
3. Server builds Gemini prompt (with optional rule constraints), calls generateContentStream()
4. Server generates a session UUID and sets X-Session-Id in the initial response headers before streaming begins
5. Server forwards each Gemini chunk to frontend via SSE; simultaneously accumulates full text for KV storage
6. On stream end, server saves { fullText, chapters[], subtitle } to KV under session:{uuid}
7. Frontend renders each chunk in real-time, parsing Markdown to detect chapters and inject 5W1H buttons

interaction.trigger: User clicks the "5W1H" button next to a chapter heading

flow.step: 1. Frontend POSTs to /api/5w1h with { sessionId, chapter }
2. Server reads session context from KV (key: session:{sessionId})
3. Server calls Gemini with chapter title + full article context to generate 5W1H JSON
4. Server returns { who, what, when, where, why, how } as JSON
5. Frontend renders the structured summary in the collapsible box below the chapter

step.title: Project Initialization & Foundation
step.tasks: - Initialize npm project with package.json, tsconfig.json, and Cloudflare Worker types - Create wrangler.toml with KV namespace binding, compatibility_date, and esbuild config - Create build.mjs for frontend bundling via esbuild - Install dependencies: youtubei.js (dev: wrangler, esbuild, typescript, @cloudflare/workers-types) - Verify wrangler dev --port 34997 starts without errors - Set up src/backend/ and src/frontend/ directory structure

step.title: YouTube Subtitle Extraction Module
step.tasks: - Verify youtubei.js runs in the Cloudflare Worker runtime — if incompatible, fall back to raw Innertube HTTP calls - Implement src/backend/youtube.ts with subtitle extraction via the chosen approach (youtubei.js or raw Innertube) - Parse YouTube URLs (youtube.com and youtu.be formats) to extract video ID - Implement language fallback chain: zh-CN → zh → en - Implement Webshare TCP Socket proxy via cloudflare:sockets connect() for captcha bypass - Implement hard-coded subtitle fallback from src/fallback-subtitles/demo.txt - Strip timestamp markers and clean subtitle text for AI input

step.title: Gemini AI Module & Prompt Templates
step.tasks: - Implement src/backend/gemini.ts — Gemini API client for gemini-3.5-flash - Implement generateContentStream() for streaming article generation - Implement generateContent() for 5W1H structured JSON summary - Create src/backend/prompts.ts with article generation prompt template - Add optional rule constraint injection (taskType, style, audience, constraint) into prompts - Create 5W1H summarization prompt template using chapter context + full article - Add error handling with graceful fallback messages on API failure

step.title: Session Storage with Cloudflare KV
step.tasks: - Implement src/backend/session.ts for KV read/write operations - Save session on article completion: key session:{uuid}, value { fullText, chapters[], subtitle }, TTL 3600s - Retrieve session by sessionId for 5W1H generation - Auto-expire sessions after 1 hour to prevent storage bloat

step.title: Worker Router & SSE Streaming Endpoints
step.tasks: - Implement src/backend/router.ts — route GET / to serve HTML page - Serve bundled static assets from public/ at /public/* - Implement POST /api/generate: subtitle extraction → Gemini streaming → SSE response with X-Session-Id - Implement POST /api/5w1h: KV context lookup → Gemini 5W1H generation → JSON response - Create src/backend/index.ts entry point wiring router + fetch handler - Handle CORS headers for cross-origin requests

step.title: Frontend: UI, SSE Client & Markdown Rendering
step.tasks: - Create HTML page with input area (YouTube URL field + custom rules text field + Generate button) - Implement CSS with design system tokens: colors (#2563eb, #ffffff, #111827, #e5e7eb), typography, responsive layout - Implement SSE client in vanilla JS — connect to /api/generate, read stream, append chunks to DOM - Implement lightweight regex Markdown parser to detect # title, ## chapters, and paragraphs - Inject [5W1H] buttons next to each ## chapter heading during streaming - Implement 5W1H collapsible summary boxes — POST /api/5w1h then render Who/What/When/Where/Why/How - Disable Generate button during active generation; show loading states - Validate YouTube URL format on input; show inline error feedback

step.title: Integration Testing & End-to-End Validation
step.tasks: - Test with demo video (xRh2sVcNXQ8): subtitle extraction → streaming article → chapter rendering - Test custom generation rules: verify AI output reflects taskType, style, audience, constraint - Test 5W1H summaries: verify correct structured JSON; verify no client re-transmission of article - Test fallback chain: simulate direct fetch failure → proxy retry → hard-coded fallback - Test error cases: invalid URL, missing subtitles, API key errors, KV unavailability - Verify responsive layout on mobile and desktop viewports

step.title: Deployment & Go-Live
step.tasks: - Create Cloudflare KV namespace via wrangler kv:namespace create - Configure Gemini API key and Webshare proxy credentials as secrets - Build frontend assets via node build.mjs - Run wrangler deploy to publish Worker to production - Verify public HTTPS access with no CORS errors - Test full flow on deployed URL with demo video

success_criteria.functionality: - YouTube URL parsing handles both youtube.com and youtu.be formats correctly - Subtitle extraction works end-to-end with the demo video (xRh2sVcNXQ8) - Subtitle language fallback chain works: zh-CN → zh → en - Streaming article generation produces structured Markdown in real-time via SSE - Custom generation rules (taskType, style, audience, constraint) observably influence AI output - Chapter detection correctly parses ## headings and injects [5W1H] buttons - 5W1H summaries return correct Who/What/When/Where/Why/How for each chapter - 5W1H endpoint uses server-side KV context — no client re-transmission of full article - Hard-coded subtitle fallback loads correctly when direct and proxy fetch both fail - URL format validation shows instant inline feedback for invalid inputs - Generate button is disabled during active generation to prevent duplicate submissions
success_criteria.user_experience: - Streaming text renders in real-time with typewriter effect — chunks appear as they are generated - 5W1H summary boxes are collapsed by default; expand smoothly on click, collapsible again - Article layout is clean and readable: clear distinction between headings, paragraphs, and chapters - Responsive layout adapts to mobile, tablet, and desktop screen widths - Loading states are shown during generation; clear error messages on failure - After generation completes, page refresh can still re-query 5W1H summaries via KV (session survives refresh)
success_criteria.technical_quality: - Clean module separation: each src/backend/*.ts file has a single responsibility - Gemini API key and Webshare credentials stored as secrets — never exposed to client - Cloudflare KV session cache auto-expires after 1 hour (TTL 3600s) - SSE streaming uses TransformStream — efficient, backpressure-aware, no memory accumulation - Subtitle fallback chain is resilient: direct → proxy → hard-coded; service never fully breaks - Gemini API failures return meaningful error messages, not raw stack traces - Frontend JS bundled via esbuild with no framework dependencies - TCP Socket proxy implementation correctly uses cloudflare:sockets connect() - No CORS errors on deployed Worker - All TypeScript code is type-safe with no `any` types except at system boundaries
success_criteria.design_polish: - Design system tokens applied consistently: #2563eb primary, #ffffff background, #111827 text, #e5e7eb borders - Typography: system-ui sans-serif at 16px base, 1.8 line-height for comfortable reading - Input elements: rounded borders, light gray background, visible focus highlight ring - Buttons: hover state change, clear disabled state (grayed out + not-allowed cursor) - Chapter cards: subtle border + padding to visually separate sections - 5W1H summary boxes: light gray background distinct from chapter content - Smooth transitions on summary box expand/collapse and button hover - Excellent color contrast for accessibility (dark text on light background)
